description:
## Goal

Route bitstrings above 64 bytes through the shared zone; keep small bitstrings inline. Introduce dispatch helpers so every read site treats `HeapKind::Bitstring` and `HeapKind::ProcBin` uniformly.

## Depends on

fz-cty.4 (deep_copy_value handles ProcBin).

## Threshold

```rust
pub const SHARED_BIN_THRESHOLD_BYTES: usize = 64;
```

Matches BEAM's refc-binary threshold. Anything ≤ 64 bytes stays inline; anything > 64 bytes goes to the shared zone via ProcBin.

## Dispatch helpers (runtime/src/fz_value.rs)

```rust
pub fn is_bitstring_like(p: *const HeapHeader) -> bool;
pub fn bitstring_bit_len(p: *const HeapHeader) -> u64;
pub fn bitstring_byte_ptr(p: *const HeapHeader) -> *const u8;
```

Each dispatches on `(*p).kind`:
- `Bitstring`: read bit_len at +16, bytes at +24.
- `ProcBin`: read shared_ptr at +16, dereference to a `&SharedBin`, return its `bit_len` and `bytes.as_ptr()`.

## Changes

### runtime/src/heap.rs
- `alloc_bitstring(bytes, bit_len)`:
  ```rust
  if bytes.len() > SHARED_BIN_THRESHOLD_BYTES {
      let shared_ptr = shared_bin_alloc(bytes, bit_len);
      return self.alloc_procbin(shared_ptr);
  }
  // existing inline path
  ```

### runtime/src/fz_value.rs
- Add `is_bitstring_like`, `bitstring_bit_len`, `bitstring_byte_ptr`.
- Update `render_bitstring` to use the helpers (replace direct `+16` / `+24` reads).
- Update the FzValue render dispatch to call `render_bitstring` for both `Bitstring` and `ProcBin` kinds.

### runtime/src/heap.rs — deep_copy_value
- The existing `Bitstring` arm stays (small inline bitstrings deep-copy as today via the inline path).
- The `ProcBin` arm (added in .4) handles the shared case.
- No new conditional logic — the two arms are independent.

### runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs
- `fz_bs_write_field`: replace `Some(HeapKind::Bitstring)` check with `is_bitstring_like`; replace `+16` and `+24` reads with helper calls.
- `fz_bs_reader_init`: same treatment.
- `fz_bs_read_field`: same treatment.

## Tests

- Allocate a 32-byte bitstring → inline Bitstring kind; mso_list empty.
- Allocate a 128-byte bitstring → ProcBin kind; mso_list has one entry; SharedBin refcount = 1.
- Render: 128-byte bitstring renders identically to a hypothetical 128-byte inline bitstring with the same bytes (use the dispatch helpers to read both).
- Bit-matching round-trip: bs_reader_init + bs_read_field over a 128-byte ProcBin source yields the expected bytes.
- Send across processes: a 128-byte bitstring sent from process A to process B uses ProcBin + retain (covered by .4 acceptance, exercised here at the alloc_bitstring level).

## Non-changes

- ir_codegen.rs: no changes; all bitstring access in code is funneled through the runtime functions above.
- Pattern matching / equality semantics are content-equal regardless of storage kind.

## Verification

- All four read sites compile and pass tests.
- Existing bitstring tests pass under both inline and ProcBin storage paths.
- Three-path parity: send + receive of a large bitstring works in interp, JIT, AOT (exercised in .6).
title: fz-shr.5: alloc_bitstring threshold + dispatch helpers
